title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 is often cited (0) as one of the most important accidents in medical history. (9) Although Fleming was actually researching staphylococci, he noticed a mysterious mold that had started killing the bacteria in his Petri dishes. (10) Such was the significance of this observation that it eventually led to the development of the world’s first true antibiotic. (11) Provided that a patient was treated early enough, infections that were once fatal could now be cured with ease. (12) Not only did penicillin save countless lives during World War II, but it also opened the door to modern pharmaceutical research. (13) Regardless of Fleming's initial discovery, it took years of further work by other scientists to stabilize the drug for mass production. (14) Not until the 1940s did penicillin become widely available to the general public, transforming healthcare forever. (15) In spite of its success, the overprescription of antibiotics has now led to the rise of resistant 'superbugs' in many hospitals. (16) Whether or not we can develop new treatments to stay ahead of these bacteria is a major challenge for modern science. This historic breakthrough reminds us that curiosity is essential.

example.text: The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 is often cited (0) as one of the most important accidents.
example.explanation: Preposition used to describe the role or status of something.

questions.explanation: Conjunction used to introduce a contrast.
Determiner used for emphasis before a noun phrase.
Conjunction meaning 'if' or 'on the condition that'.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' correlative conjunction.
Part of the fixed phrase 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure requiring inversion ('did penicillin become').
Part of the prepositional linker 'In spite of'.
Conjunction used to introduce a doubt between options.
